start_urls_note: Print-edition archive (paginated /print-edition/page/N/, ~8 pages). WordPress front-end aggregates all print articles.

navigation.volume_url_pattern: none (flat paginated post listing tagged volume-{N})
navigation.archive_path: homepage -> Print Edition -> /print-edition/page/{N}/ lists articles -> article detail page -> 'Download the Full Article' button -> digitalcommons.law.uw.edu/wlr/vol{N}/iss{M}/{P}/
navigation.total_volumes_estimated: 8 listing pages spanning at least vol 100-101 (sampled); full archive deeper
navigation.pdf_location: article-detail-page (PDF actually hosted on Digital Commons backend digitalcommons.law.uw.edu/wlr/)
navigation.notes: WordPress (Salient theme) front-end at washingtonlawreview.org. The 'Download the Full Article' anchor on each article page links to a Digital Commons landing page (digitalcommons.law.uw.edu/wlr/volN/issM/P/), which then serves the actual PDF via context.cgi. There is also a parallel sitemap (washington_law_review.json) targeting the DC backend directly via DigitalCommonsBaseAdapter; this WP seed is complementary front-end coverage so that current-issue WordPress posts are also crawled. The homepage rejects bare curl with 403 unless a real browser UA is sent. Direct curl to www.washingtonlawreview.org returns 403; canonical host without www works. Smoke (2026-05-02): WordPressAcademicBaseAdapter discovered a wp-content/uploads style-guide PDF (494KB, 2024) before traversing into article pages; primary article PDFs require the WP adapter to follow article_url and then chase the digitalcommons download button. Volume not captured by adapter — needs selector tuning (volume comes from post tag like 'tag-volume-101'). Companion DC sitemap (washington_law_review.json) is the authoritative full-archive source.
